statement.markdown: Given a Trie data structure, write a function to insert a string into the Trie. The function should return true if the string is inserted successfully, and false otherwise. Note that the Trie is case-insensitive.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a string `word` to be inserted into the Trie.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return a boolean indicating whether the insertion was successful.
statement.notes: The Trie is initially empty. The input string is guaranteed to be non-empty and contains only lowercase English letters.

examples.explanation: The string 'apple' should be inserted into the Trie successfully.
The single character 'a' should be inserted into the Trie successfully.

hints.text: Always insert a character into the correct child node of the current node.
Make sure to update the end-of-word indicator when inserting a new string.

editorial.title: Fixing Trie Insertion Edge Case
editorial.intuition: The key to solving this problem is to understand how the Trie data structure works and how to insert a string into it.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute force approach would involve creating a new Trie node for each character in the string and then updating the end-of-word indicator.
editorial.betterSolution: We can use a recursive approach to insert the string into the Trie.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves using a recursive approach with a Trie node class and a recursive function to handle the insertion.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by showing that it correctly inserts the string into the Trie.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the solution using the provided test cases.
commonMistakes: Failing to update the end-of-word indicator
Incorrectly handling the insertion of a new character
